lucidBlocksBeginnerGuide.title: Slime Seas Codes
lucidBlocksBeginnerGuide.intro: Redeem the latest Slime Seas codes for race rerolls, potion boosts, and chest keys. Use this section to avoid expired entries and claim rewards quickly.

steps.title: HUBISLAND (Active)
steps.description: Rewards: 3 Race Rerolls and a 30-minute Superior Gold Potion.

steps.title: SLIMEPIECE (Active)
steps.description: Rewards: 2 Race Rerolls and 30 minutes of Superior EXP.

steps.title: FREEBOOSTS (Active)
steps.description: Rewards: 30 minutes of Greater Power and 30 minutes of Greater Luck.

steps.title: 1MILLIONVISITS (Active)
steps.description: Rewards: 3 Fabled Chest Keys and 30 minutes of Greater Luck.

steps.title: Recent Expired Codes
steps.description: RELEASE, 1KLIKESWHOO, YAY5000, COMBATCHANGES, and 2500LIKES are currently expired.

steps.title: How to Redeem Slime Seas Codes
steps.description: Finish tutorial, open Shop, paste code in the bottom input box, and press Submit.

quickTips: Check official Discord and X after each update for fresh Slime Seas code drops.
Copy and paste codes exactly because spacing and capitalization must match.
Redeem active codes before reroll sessions so race planning is more efficient.
If a code fails, verify whether it has already moved to the expired list first.

lucidBlocksStorageAndInventory.title: Slime Seas Beginner Guide
lucidBlocksStorageAndInventory.intro: Follow this Slime Seas early route to move from Starting Forest into Witch Tower with fewer detours.

solutions.name: Start in Starting Forest
solutions.description: Farm early enemies, collect Slime Residue, and build your first gear baseline before pushing further.

solutions.name: Lock Core Controls Early
solutions.description: Stabilize your rhythm with M1 attacks, Q dash, F block, and 1-6 skill usage in short combat loops.

solutions.name: Push Weapon Mastery First
solutions.description: Mastery unlocks the real combat kit, so focusing one style early gives faster strength than constant swapping.

solutions.name: Set Waypoint and Move to Witch Tower
solutions.description: After first boss readiness, set your waypoint and transition to Witch Tower for higher-power progression.

managementTips: Use wooden gear benchmarks to set realistic early targets before chasing rare drops.
Run quests and mastery together so level gains always improve your combat options.
Prepare around level 15 before entering your first major boss window.
Use waypoints to cut travel time and increase combat session efficiency.

lucidBlocksQualiaAndBaseBuilding.title: Slime Seas Race Tier List
lucidBlocksQualiaAndBaseBuilding.intro: Races in Slime Seas decide how your build scales with cooldowns, mobility, survivability, and weapon bonuses. Use this tier list to answer the reroll question quickly: lock it, save it, or spin again.

cards.name: Lunarian
cards.rerollAdvice: Lock in if you want Katana or a fast general build

cards.name: Elf
highlights: High spam potential from lower cooldowns
cards.rerollAdvice: Strong value keep if you do not want to chase Fabled or Legendary

cards.name: Shadowborn
drawbacks: Fragile because of the HP penalty
cards.rerollAdvice: Keep only if you are committed to Katana

cards.name: Skypiean
cards.rerollAdvice: Fine early keep, easy reroll later

cards.name: Fishman
bestFor: Greatsword if you lack better rolls
cards.rerollAdvice: Use only if your route makes water travel matter

cards.name: Human
highlights: Small leveling help at the start
cards.rerollAdvice: Reroll as soon as you can

highlights: Lock Dragonborn when you want the best all-around race value.
Keep Lunarian for fast Katana routes with durable movement.
Only hold Shadowborn if your plan is Katana-only burst gameplay.
Treat Human as a temporary roll and reroll at the first opportunity.

lucidBlocksWorldRegions.title: Slime Seas Best Weapons
lucidBlocksWorldRegions.intro: The best Slime Seas weapons are the ones that scale hard, are realistic to obtain, and carry your current progression stage. This grid separates true endgame goals from practical bridge picks.

regions.name: Dominion Brand
regions.bestFor: Short boss windows and top-end burst
regions.access: Dropped by the Demon Lord and requires the Demon Lord Title to equip
regions.whyPick: Hardest-hitting option in the current weapon pool and a clear endgame target.

regions.name: Slime Sword
regions.bestFor: Best overall DPS and sustained boss damage
regions.access: Craft with Demon Lord Title, Slime Core, Slime Remnants, Tempest Seals, and Sage Pulse Gems
regions.whyPick: Safest late-game build-around weapon when you want one strong all-mode option.

regions.name: Void Blade
regions.whyPick: Comes online earlier than top-end options while keeping excellent speed.

regions.name: Thunder Staff
regions.whyPick: Strong magic-side pick when you value reach and room clear over melee pressure.

regions.name: Frost Dagger
regions.whyPick: Fastest attack speed in this list, making it a clean bridge while farming upgrades.

regions.name: Iron Slime Hammer
regions.access: High drop availability in early routes
regions.whyPick: One of the easiest practical upgrades to replace starter gear.

lucidBlocksCreaturesAndEnemies.title: Slime Seas Weapon Mastery Guide
lucidBlocksCreaturesAndEnemies.intro: Mastery in Slime Seas decides your skill chain, race pairing, and farming rhythm. Choose your lane early to avoid wasting rerolls and materials.

creatures.role: Top PvE and boss-farming mastery with fast single-target pressure
creatures.whyPick: Best starting mastery if your goal is fast boss damage and quick-hit combo flow.

creatures.role: Best beginner route for AoE farming and all-round PvE consistency
creatures.whyPick: Smoothest early-to-midgame lane because weapon availability and AoE both stay strong.

creatures.role: PvP control route with grab pressure and mobility-focused skill play
creatures.whyPick: Choose Scythe when you care about control sequences and PvP pressure windows.

creatures.role: Gamepass Greatsword stance with stronger PvP opening pressure
creatures.whyPick: Worth buying only if Greatsword already fits your style and you want an aggressive opener.

lucidBlocksMobilityGear.title: Slime Seas World Progression Guide
lucidBlocksMobilityGear.intro: Slime Seas opens up quickly, but world gates, quest routes, and boss prep are easy to scatter. This timeline gives you one practical route from spawn to late-game farming.

items.target: Reach around level 15 before the first boss push
focus: Learn controls and basic quest loops
Farm starter materials and first weapon upgrades
items.leaveWhen: You can clear early quests cleanly and are ready for your first island move.

items.unlock: Progress after early quests and first gear upgrades
focus: Push the first visible power gate
Collect the next layer of crafting materials
items.leaveWhen: You can hold the 100 power checkpoint comfortably and clear tower loops smoothly.

items.unlock: Optional detour once you are around level 30
focus: Secure travel upgrades before long routes
Use stronger early weapons to shorten repeats
items.leaveWhen: Your Drake is hatched and travel speed is no longer slowing your core route.

items.unlock: Midgame questline after the early islands
items.target: World 5+ material routing and 7 Slime Pieces questline
focus: Prepare materials for legendary weapon paths
items.leaveWhen: You have stable Slime Piece routes and can start Demon Lord and legendary prep.

items.unlock: Level 40+, Epic-tier weapon, Demon Lord Title, and active guild support
items.target: Late-game boss farming and Ascension materials
focus: Run boss protocol loops with minimal downtime
Push late-game crafting and prestige systems
Build toward top-end PvE and PvP loadouts
items.leaveWhen: You are farming Ascension Level 8 materials and building complete endgame setups.

unlockMilestones: Clear Starting Forest and push your first boss gate
Hold Witch Tower power checkpoints without route stalls
Finish Crimson Ruins travel detour with a hatched Drake
Stabilize World 5+ material loops and Slime Piece routing
Start late-game boss protocols for Ascension material farming

lucidBlocksFarmingAndGrowth.title: Slime Seas Bosses and Drops
lucidBlocksFarmingAndGrowth.intro: Use this Slime Seas boss table to route progression by level gates, drop priorities, and practical farming value from your first major boss to the current endgame chase.

bosses.topDrops: Wing Flaps (35.5%), Iron Novice Pants (23.66%), Iron Novice Chestplate (17.75%), Slime Lord Pants (12.42%), Slime Lord Chestplate (8.87%), Slime Katana (1.77%)
bosses.bestFor: First boss farm and early progression
bosses.fightNote: Katana-style boss and the easiest major boss to start farming.

bosses.topDrops: Sinbad's Tail, Copper Novice Greaves, Copper Novice Chestplate, Thorn Greaves, Thorn Chestplate, Thornblade
bosses.fightNote: This arena does not support the common outside-arena setup.

bosses.topDrops: Ogre Horns (41.58%), Steel Adventurer Pants (27.72%), Steel Adventurer Chestplate (20.79%), Apprentice Plated Greaves (4.85%), Doomblade (0.51%), Gilded Leviathan (0.51%), Monarch's Scythe (0.51%)
bosses.bestFor: Crafting materials and late mid-game farming
bosses.fightNote: Best common-drop materials boss; trio raids double total boss HP.

bosses.topDrops: Sigil Fragment (42.02%), Shadow Steel Greaves (28.01%), Shadow Steel Chestplate (21.01%), Igris Helmet (4.2%), Igris Greaves (2.8%), Igris Chestplate (1.4%), Amethyst Avenger (0.17%), Hollow Wraith Blade (0.17%), Necroshade (0.17%)
bosses.bestFor: End-game progression and rare weapon chasing
bosses.fightNote: Current endgame boss with the heaviest rare-drop chase.

farmPriorities: Start with Demon Lord Rima for your first stable armor and weapon farm window.
Use King Sinbad as your bridge to stronger mid-game Greatsword routes.
Farm Ogre Lord when you need high-volume materials plus late mid-game drops.
Push Monarch Statue when your build is ready for endgame rare-weapon chasing.

lucidBlocksBestEarlyUnlocks.title: Slime Seas Pets Guide
lucidBlocksBestEarlyUnlocks.intro: The Slime Seas pet loop is simple and practical: secure the Drake Egg, hatch it through slime kills, and use the Forest Drake as your first high-value travel upgrade.

steps.heading: Farm the Crimson Demon
steps.body: Start at Crimson Ruins Island and farm the Crimson Demon mini-boss. The fight is level 50, and most players begin the grind around level 30.

steps.heading: Get the Drake Egg Drop
steps.body: The Drake Egg is commonly tracked as a 9% drop from the Crimson Demon. Reposition often because split minions can pressure from multiple angles.

steps.heading: Hatch by Killing Slimes
steps.body: After the egg drops, keep clearing slimes through your normal route. The hatch process advances from slime kills, not a separate incubation menu.

steps.heading: Claim the Forest Drake
steps.body: The current live pet route culminates in the Forest Drake, which functions as your first practical mount.

steps.heading: Use the Mount for Routing
steps.body: In the current Slime Seas loop, the biggest pet value is travel speed between islands and route checkpoints.

steps.heading: Keep It Active for XP Growth
steps.body: The pet gains experience as you continue farming. Hatch completion is usually around 1 to 3 hours depending on level, weapon strength, and clear pace.

progressNotes: Begin the Slime Seas pet grind when your core combat route is already stable.
Link egg farming to your current leveling path to avoid low-value detours.
Treat the Forest Drake as a routing upgrade first, then a long-term progression layer.
Maintain regular slime clears so hatch progress continues without dedicated downtime.

lucidBlocksAchievementTracker.title: Slime Seas Merchant Locations
lucidBlocksAchievementTracker.intro: The most practical Slime Seas merchant data is route-based: rotate Forest to Desert coverage, check spawned merchants first, and use island waypoints to shorten every recheck loop.

routes.whatConfirmed: Public merchant-location coverage starts in the Forest side of Slime Seas.
routes.howToUse: Begin vendor sweeps here when running an early-side route reset.
routes.whyItMatters: This stop anchors one confirmed end of the merchant path.

routes.whatConfirmed: Public merchant-location coverage extends through the Desert side.
routes.howToUse: Treat this as the far end of your full merchant sweep once later islands unlock.
routes.whyItMatters: This stop marks the other confirmed end of the known vendor route.

routes.whatConfirmed: Merchants can spawn live in Slime Seas sessions.
routes.howToUse: Check for active spawned merchants before sailing the complete route.
routes.whyItMatters: Spawn checks can cut the full vendor loop when timing lines up.

routes.whatConfirmed: Each island supports waypoint-based return travel.
routes.howToUse: Set waypoints on repeat-visit islands, then chain fast merchant checks in sequence.
routes.whyItMatters: Waypoints reduce downtime and make merchant revisits efficient.

routeChecklist: Start from the Forest end and finish at the Desert end when doing full sweeps.
Run spawned-merchant checks first before committing to long travel loops.
Pair vendor checks with active quest turn-ins to remove dead travel time.
Refresh waypoints whenever your Slime Seas progression route changes.

lucidBlocksSingleplayerAndPlatformFAQ.title: Slime Seas Skills Guide
lucidBlocksSingleplayerAndPlatformFAQ.intro: Slime Seas skills are mastery-driven. Start with Dash for universal movement, then prioritize mastery 1 and mastery 5 skills that define your PvE and PvP rhythm.

skills.summary: Dash is the baseline Slime Seas movement skill and the first input habit every player should build for boss dodges and route speed.
Flashblade Surge is a core Katana opener in Slime Seas and sets up fast repeat pressure for PvE chains.
Thousand Slashes is the signature Katana finisher, widely used for single-target pressure and safe burst windows.
Death's Grasp gives Slime Seas Scythe routes their control identity and starts most pressure chains.
Void Surge closes Scythe chains and is the key mastery finisher for control-heavy PvP sequences.
Earth Breaker is the first Greatsword mastery skill and a major reason Slime Seas Greatsword routes feel smooth for farming.
Spiraling Slash is Greatsword's signature finisher and supports wide-area clears during repeat material loops.
Impaling Thrust is the standout Berserker opener in Slime Seas and starts high-pressure burst sequences.

trainingNotes: Use Slime Seas Dash on cooldown discipline, not panic input, to control spacing.
Commit to one mastery line early so skill unlocks arrive before route difficulty spikes.
Build openers and finishers in pairs to keep combo flow consistent under pressure.
Re-evaluate your skill sequence when shifting from leveling routes to boss or PvP focus.

lucidBlocksSteamDeckAndController.title: Slime Seas PvP Guide
lucidBlocksSteamDeckAndController.subtitle: Slime Seas PvP builds, race pairings, and duel flow
lucidBlocksSteamDeckAndController.intro: Start every Slime Seas duel with movement discipline: Q for Dash, F for Block, Shift for Mouse Lock, and 1-4 for weapon skills. Current build data points to Scythe as a stable PvP control option, Katana as the high-burst duelist choice, and Greatsword or Berserker as the pressure-heavy bruiser path.

items.summary: Use dash spacing and block timing to force whiffs before committing to a full skill chain.
keyNumbers: Open with movement discipline before skill commitment
Keep close-range strings inside Mouse Lock
duelTips: Open with movement first, then commit once the opponent spends mobility.
Stay in Mouse Lock during close trades so short-range follow-ups stay on target.
Block front-loaded swings, then answer with your highest-value control or burst skill.

items.summary: Best current PvP mastery for players who want stable confirms and strong chase pressure.
duelTips: Use Death's Grasp to start the exchange and force a guaranteed follow-up.
Djinn pairs cleanly with Scythe through cooldown speed, +20% Scythe damage, and +25% dash distance.
Dragonborn is a safer premium pick when you want Scythe damage and Greatsword flexibility.

items.summary: Best for duelists who want fast strings, strong boss carryover, and sharp punish windows.
keyNumbers: Mastery 5 burst: Thousand Slashes = 2,700 PvP damage
Shadowborn bonuses: +35% Katana, +40% dash distance, +15% speed
duelTips: Shadowborn has the highest Katana ceiling but trades away health and off-weapon damage.
Lunarian is a cleaner all-around Katana pick with speed, health, and fire scaling together.
Play for whiff punishes and short kill windows over long face-tank trades.

items.summary: Pick this path if you want wider swings, easier wave clear, and sturdier melee pressure.
keyNumbers: Greatsword Mastery 5: Spiraling Slash = 1,700 PvP damage
duelTips: Greatsword is the best free all-rounder with strong AoE and smooth progression value.
Oni is the best Rare pairing for Greatsword with +15% health and +20% Greatsword damage.
Berserker is a premium Greatsword stance for players who already like the Greatsword rhythm.

lucidBlocksSettingsAndAccessibility.title: Slime Seas Items and Materials
lucidBlocksSettingsAndAccessibility.subtitle: Slime Seas key items, materials, and first chase drops
lucidBlocksSettingsAndAccessibility.intro: Use this Slime Seas module as a quick lookup sheet for starter weapons, boss materials, and first real upgrade targets. The current indexed route covers Starting Forest, Demon Lord Rima, Ogre Lord, and Monarch Statue progression checkpoints.

items.whyItMatters: Baseline early weapon and the simplest reference point for damage checks.
Core early material that appears in the first farm loop.
Most common Rima drop and part of the first serious boss farm.
The first standout boss weapon jump for Katana-focused progression.
Top mid-game material farm and a high-value crafting target.
Endgame progression material tied to the current highest-tier boss loop.

lucidBlocksUpdatesAndPatchNotes.title: Slime Seas Damage Calculator
lucidBlocksUpdatesAndPatchNotes.subtitle: Slime Seas quick damage checks and item comparisons
lucidBlocksUpdatesAndPatchNotes.intro: The live Slime Seas tool hub exposes a damage calculator, item compare view, upgrade simulator, and build planner. This module focuses on race selection, weapon selection, enchant level, and quick stat deltas between starter and first-boss gear.

rows.note: The compare view highlights the starter weapon over a raw material.

rows.comparison: Legendary Slime Katana vs Wooden Katana
rows.note: This is the first major weapon jump most early boss farmers track.

lucidBlocksCrashFixAndTroubleshooting.title: Slime Seas Reroll Probability
lucidBlocksCrashFixAndTroubleshooting.subtitle: Slime Seas target-race odds for rerolls and code stockpiles
lucidBlocksCrashFixAndTroubleshooting.intro: The current Slime Seas race pool has 9 races, with Human at 48%, Fabled targets at 2% each, and Dragonborn and Shadowborn at 0.25% each. Hit chance follows 1 - (1 - p)^n, so small reroll stockpiles behave very differently from long-hunt sessions.

starterCodeBundle.title: Slime Seas Starter Code Bundle
starterCodeBundle.summary: HUBISLAND gives 3 Race Rerolls and SLIMEPIECE gives 2 Race Rerolls, for a 5-reroll starter stockpile.

faq.title: Slime Seas Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Slime Seas

questions.question: Is Slime Seas a Roblox anime RPG?
questions.answer: Yes. Slime Seas is a Roblox anime RPG focused on world progression, weapon mastery, boss routes, and PvP-ready builds.

questions.question: How do Slime Seas codes help progression?
questions.answer: Slime Seas codes provide rerolls, potion boosts, and keys that accelerate race planning and early-to-mid progression.

questions.question: Where can I find official Slime Seas updates?
questions.answer: Use the official Slime Seas Discord, X account, Trello board, and Roblox Group for announcements and roadmap notes.

questions.question: What should new players focus on first in Slime Seas?
questions.answer: New Slime Seas players should lock core controls, focus one weapon mastery route, and follow world checkpoints with waypoint setup.

questions.question: How should I plan Slime Seas race rerolls?
questions.answer: Plan Slime Seas rerolls around probability, current race utility, and available reroll stock instead of chasing random outcomes.

questions.question: Does Slime Seas include PvP progression?
questions.answer: Yes. Slime Seas includes PvP, and build tuning around race passives, mastery skills, and spacing discipline improves consistency.

bosses.whatIs: What is the Slime Seas Bosses category?
bosses.whatIsDescription: The Slime Seas Bosses category is dedicated to encounter execution and reward planning. It combines boss mechanics, damage windows, drop-rate strategy, and farm loops for consistent progression gains.
bosses.why: Why focus on Slime Seas bosses?

whySteps.step1Description: Use mechanics and timing guides to reduce failed runs and recovery time.
whySteps.step2Description: Prioritize bosses and loot pools that best fit your current build upgrades.
whySteps.step3Description: Convert repeated boss runs into predictable gear and material growth.
